data.text/markdown:
Chemical Reaction Kinetics Simulator

Explore how reactant concentration changes over time for zeroth, first, and second order reactions. Each section provides the rate law and integrated equations, interactive parameter sliders, and two characteristic plots that update in real time.

0th Order Reactions

In a zeroth-order reaction the rate is completely independent of the reactant concentration — it proceeds at a constant rate until the reactant is fully consumed.

Rate Law

||[\frac{d[A]}{dt} = -k||]

Integrated Rate Law

||[\boxed{[A](t) = [A]_0 - k\,t}||]A plot of ||([A]||) vs ||(t||) is a straight line with slope ||(= -k||) and ||(y||)-intercept ||(= [A]_0||). The reaction ends at ||(t_\text{end} = [A]_0 / k||).

Half-Life

||[t_{1/2} = \frac{[A]_0}{2k}||]The half-life decreases with each successive interval because ||([A]_0||) is smaller after each half-life.

Units of ||(k||)

||[[k] = \mathrm{mol\,L^{-1}\,s^{-1}}||]
Characteristic graph: ||([A]||) vs ||(t||) is linear. The Rate vs Time plot is a flat horizontal line at ||(y = k||).

data.text/html: ⚙️ Adjust Parameters — 0th Order

data.text/markdown:
1st Order Reactions

In a first-order reaction the rate is directly proportional to the reactant concentration.

Rate Law

||[\frac{d[A]}{dt} = -k[A]||]

Integrated Rate Law

||[\boxed{[A](t) = [A]_0\,e^{-kt}}||]Taking the natural logarithm yields a linear form: ||[\ln[A] = \ln[A]_0 - kt||]A plot of ||(\ln[A]||) vs ||(t||) is a straight line with slope ||(= -k||).

Half-Life

||[t_{1/2} = \frac{\ln 2}{k} \approx \frac{0.693}{k}||]The half-life is constant — it does not depend on ||([A]_0||). This is the hallmark of first-order kinetics. Radioactive decay is the classic example.

Units of ||(k||)

||[[k] = \mathrm{s^{-1}}||]
Characteristic graph: ||([A]||) vs ||(t||) is an exponential decay. ||(\ln[A]||) vs ||(t||) is perfectly linear.

data.text/html: ⚙️ Adjust Parameters — 1st Order

data.text/markdown:
2nd Order Reactions

In a second-order reaction the rate depends on the square of the reactant concentration.

Rate Law

||[\frac{d[A]}{dt} = -k[A]^2||]

Integrated Rate Law

||[\boxed{\frac{1}{[A](t)} = \frac{1}{[A]_0} + kt}||]A plot of ||(1/[A]||) vs ||(t||) is a straight line with slope ||(= k||) and intercept ||(= 1/[A]_0||).

Half-Life

||[t_{1/2} = \frac{1}{k[A]_0}||]The half-life increases as the reaction proceeds — the reaction slows dramatically at low concentrations. Each successive half-life is twice as long as the previous one.

Units of ||(k||)

||[[k] = \mathrm{L\,mol^{-1}\,s^{-1}}||]
Characteristic graph: ||([A]||) vs ||(t||) decays hyperbolically. ||(1/[A]||) vs ||(t||) is perfectly linear.

data.text/html: ⚙️ Adjust Parameters — 2nd Order
